sourceEvidence.note: The base @colbymchenry/codegraph package ships only npm-shim.js + dist/**/*.d.ts (the CLI JS runs from a per-platform optionalDependency bundle). Every --json shape below was read from the COMPILED CLI source at package/lib/dist/bin/codegraph.js inside the pinned win32-x64 1.4.1 bundle — the exact JSON.stringify(...) call for each command's `if (options.json)` branch. Entity shapes cross-checked against dist/types.d.ts (Node/Edge/GraphStats/SearchResult interfaces). Sample DATA is synthetic-but-realistic; STRUCTURE (field names/types) mirrors upstream verbatim.

commandShapeSource.status: codegraph.js L844-882 (initialized:true branch): { initialized, version, projectPath, indexPath, lastIndexed(ISO|null), fileCount, nodeCount, edgeCount, dbSizeBytes, backend, journalMode, nodesByKind, languages[], pendingChanges{added,modified,removed}, worktreeMismatch(null|{worktreeRoot,indexRoot}), index{builtWithVersion,builtWithExtractionVersion,currentExtractionVersion,reindexRecommended,state,pendingRefs} }
commandShapeSource.files: codegraph.js L1370-1377: array of { path, language, nodeCount, size } — NO module membership (files keyed by path)
commandShapeSource.query: codegraph.js L1009-1010: JSON.stringify(results) where results is SearchResult[] = [{ node: Node, score, highlights? }]; Node carries the upstream entity id in `id` (dist/types.d.ts)
commandShapeSource.callers: codegraph.js L1718-1719: { symbol, callers: [{ name, kind, filePath, startLine }] } — rows carry NO entity id
commandShapeSource.callees: codegraph.js L1788-1789: { symbol, callees: [{ name, kind, filePath, startLine }] } — rows carry NO entity id
commandShapeSource.impact: codegraph.js L1864-1871: { symbol, depth, nodeCount, edgeCount, affected: [{ name, kind, filePath, startLine }] }
commandShapeSource.help: commander auto-generated help from program.name('codegraph') + each .command()/.description() (codegraph.js L186-188, L520-2246)
commandShapeSource.node: codegraph.js L1266 `node [name]` has NO --json option — opaque text (same output as codegraph_node MCP tool)
commandShapeSource.explore: codegraph.js L1053 `explore <query...>` has NO --json option — opaque text (same output as codegraph_explore MCP tool)

supportsPositionalSeparatorEvidence: codegraph.js requires commander (L64: require('commander')) and calls program.parse() (L2246) with NO enablePositionalOptions()/passThroughOptions()/storeOptionsAsProperties() override. Commander always honors `--` as the end-of-options separator (subsequent tokens become positional operands); this default cannot be disabled. Therefore `codegraph query -- <arg-with-leading-dash>` is supported.
